tours.title: Blue Ice Experience - 3.5-hour Skaftafell Glacier Hike
highlights: Walk on an Outlet Glacier of Vatnajökull Europe’s largest ice cap
Small Group Experience– Enjoy a more personal and immersive adventure
Expert Glacier Guides – Led by certified professionals
Beyond Standard Routes – Venture far beyond the crowded areas
Stunning Blue Ice Formations – Witness deep crevasses, ice ridges, and blue ice
Learn About Glaciology & Climate Change – Gain insights into the glacier’s geology
Easy & Accessible – Suitable for beginners with no prior glacier hiking experience required.
needToKnow: Meet on Location – Arrive at Skaftafell Base Camp 20 minutes before departure.
Not for Pregnant Women – Due to the physical nature of the hike, this tour is not recommended for pregnant participants.
Fitness Level – Suitable for beginners but requires walking on uneven, icy terrain.
What to Wear – Waterproof hiking boots with ankle support required. Wear warm, waterproof, layered clothing.
Weather Policy – Runs in most conditions. In extreme weather, the tour may be modified, rescheduled or cancelled with full refund.

Complimentary ticket for the standard Northern Lights tour (RE63) if the lights are not visible.
highlights: Sky Lagoon Saman Pass with 7 Step Skjól Ritual included

tours.title: Volcano Express Iceland
highlights: Feel real earthquakes and volcanic heat
Fly over lava fields and erupting craters
See Iceland’s landscapes transform before your eyes

tours.title: Volcano Shuttle
tours.summary: volcano Super Jeep fagradalsfjall
highlights: Ride in a specially outfitted Super Jeep with high clearance and serious off-road muscle
Reach scenic viewpoints rarely accessible by foot or standard vehicles
Marvel at panoramic views over lava flows from 2021, 2023, and 2024
Snap unforgettable photos and, where safe, get close enough to touch the newly cooled lava
Gain insight into Iceland’s dynamic landscape from expert local guides
No hiking involved — just drive, stop, explore, and be amazed

tours.title: Volcano Valley ATV Tour Near Reykjavík – 2.5 Hours
tours.summary: Discover volcanic landscapes near Reykjavík on a 2.5-hour ATV tour through lava fields, valleys and geothermal areas.
highlights: 2.5-hour ATV ride in volcanic landscapes near Reykjavík
Explore Svínahraun lava field and Jósepsdalur valley
Views of Hengill, Bláfjöll and surrounding mountains
Ride through black lava terrain and open valleys
Visit geothermal areas with steam vents and hot ground
Taste traditional Icelandic hot spring rye bread
Multiple scenic stops along the route
needToKnow: ### What’s Not Included
Extra warm clothing (recommended)
Single rider fee (if applicable)
Valid driver’s license required to drive an ATV
Two persons share one ATV (single rider upgrade available)
No prior experience needed
Tour starts from base near Reykjavík (self-drive required)
Dress according to weather conditions

tours.title: Walk with a Viking - Reykjavik Walking Tour - Small group
included: Live tour commentary provided in English
For all ages but it’s a 2 hour walk (The distance walked is roughly 3 km or 2 miles)
highlights: We will also share hidden, local spots with you like a visit to a genuine Elf home and other local gems – things you’d miss if you didn’t k…

tours.title: Whale Watching & Whales of Iceland Exhibition
Whale Watching From Húsavík
tours.summary: Enjoy a classic whale watching tour in Skjálfandi Bay, surrounded by scenic mountains and rich marine life.
included: Entry/admission fee DEPARTURE_TAX Goods and services tax
Warm overalls and raincoats if needed
needToKnow: The tour is approximately 3 hours
For your safety and comfort, we reserve the right to change schedules, even with short notice, should weather or other conditions change
The tour is approximately 3 hours
Exluded Food and drinks
